EvidenceGrade.description: Evidence-strength grade (Methodology §3.3). Drives uncertainty (§7), not the point score.
EvidenceGrade.title: EvidenceGrade

MaturityLevel.description:
The four maturity levels (Methodology §3.1).

The numeric ``index`` is for computation only; clients see the label. These four indices
are the ONLY legal subcomponent indices — any other value is a contract violation, not a
datum to clamp or round (ADR-0001 §1).
MaturityLevel.title: MaturityLevel

NonScoreState.description:
First-class non-score states (Methodology §3.2). Never imputed, never scored as zero.

An unassessed subcomponent contributes to NO score — the empty-module → q_m = 0.0
behaviour of the prototype (defect D9) is prohibited by construction.
NonScoreState.title: NonScoreState

description:
One subcomponent's rating: a maturity level OR a first-class non-score state, never both.

Modelling this as an exclusive union is how "Not Assessed" stays distinct from a score of
zero (defect D9) — an unassessed subcomponent carries `state`, not `level`, and contributes
to no computation.
title: SubcomponentRating
